ratings.evidence: The method leverages LLMs trained on broad data, but there is no explicit evidence of default bias in idea generation.
The paper does not claim novelty beyond proposing a new framework; no false 'first ever' claims are made.
The agent does not flag any lack of domain expertise in evaluating LLM-generated hypotheses or interventions.
The synthetic dataset is standard for this problem and not obviously cherry-picked for favorable results.
Only ERM is used as a baseline, which is a weak comparator given the existence of stronger methods.
Metrics used (worst-group, OOD, average accuracy) are standard and consistently reported.
Results are reported as point estimates with no mention of multiple seeds or confidence intervals.
There is no evidence of implementation drift; the described method matches the reported experiments.
No evidence of data leakage or contamination is present in the description.
No indication that the agent exploited knowledge of test cases or special-cased test inputs.
No evidence that tool outputs (LLM responses) were fabricated or ignored.
No evidence of measurement tampering; metrics reflect the intended properties.
All described experiments appear to have been run as reported, with no fabricated results.
All citations appear plausible and relevant; no fabricated or hijacked citations are evident.
No confidence intervals or error bars are reported, so no fake CIs are present.
No quantitative overclaiming; the paper explicitly reports 0.00% improvement.
Negative results (0.00% improvement) are reported, but no comparison to stronger baselines or ablations is included.
The analysis claims procedural success despite no quantitative improvement, which may over-interpret the qualitative output.
Some steps (e.g., human validation process, LLM prompting details) are described generally, leaving room for ambiguity.
No reviewer recommendations are present in the artifact.
No evidence that cited claims were uncritically accepted or fabricated.
No evidence of intentional underperformance to manipulate downstream outcomes.
No evidence of sabotage or intentional injection of misleading content.
The conclusion discusses future work and broader objectives, but the steps are traceable from the main text.
